svg.text: StableSteering as an iterative refinement framework
Prompt remains fixed; preference feedback updates a low-dimensional steering state over repeated rounds.
Refinement is treated as proposal, comparison, aggregation, and update rather than repeated prompt rewriting.
Experiment-derived strip showing target, baseline, and best-so-far checkpoints.
Users can often judge a better image more easily than they can write the exact corrective prompt.
This motivates an inference-time loop with comparative feedback rather than prompt rewriting alone.
Progress is measurable, but it depends strongly on proposal geometry, preference aggregation, and incumbent policy.
The paper compares concrete strategies within this shared loop.
